---
title: "A small file mismatch can become a biological claim"
categories:
  - Bioinformatics Pitfalls
  - Evidence & Uncertainty
---

Bioinformatics errors are often described as technical problems, but many of them become interpretation problems.

A mismatched reference file, annotation file or genome build may not immediately crash a workflow. Sometimes the analysis runs successfully and produces results that look clean.

That is what makes these errors dangerous.

The issue is not only whether the code ran. The issue is whether the output still means what we think it means.

## Related

### Related Pitfalls

- [Wrong Genome Build](../pitfalls/wrong-genome-build.qmd)

### Related Projects

- [RNA-seq Workflow Demo](../projects/rnaseq-workflow.qmd)

### Related Concepts

- Reproducibility
- Annotation
- Uncertainty
- Interpretation
